title: Day 07 Interview Prep — Week 1 Cumulative
description: Cumulative interview practice across all Week 1 topics — answer as if the machine coding round just ended.

questions.question: Explain JSX vs HTML in 30 seconds.
questions.sampleAnswer: JSX looks like HTML but compiles to JavaScript. I use className not class, embed expressions with curly braces, and close all tags. It describes UI declaratively inside React components.

questions.question: Props vs state — quick distinction.
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Day 02 — must be automatic by Day 07.
questions.sampleAnswer: Props are passed in from parent to configure a component. State is owned inside and changes over time. Login email is state; onSubmit callback might be a prop if form is reusable.

questions.question: Why keys in lists?
questions.sampleAnswer: Keys help React track which list item is which across updates. Stable ids prevent wrong DOM reuse. I avoid index keys when items can be deleted or reordered.

questions.question: Explain useEffect and dependency array.
questions.sampleAnswer: useEffect runs after paint to sync with external systems like APIs. Dependency array lists what triggers re-run. Empty array runs on mount. I include values read inside the effect. Cleanup aborts stale fetches.
followUps: You might not need an effect?

questions.question: Nested routes and Outlet — explain with dashboard example.
questions.sampleAnswer: DashboardLayout wraps sidebar and navbar. Child routes for profile and settings render inside Outlet. URL is /profile but chrome stays. Link navigates client-side without document reload.

questions.question: What is a custom hook and give an example from Week 1.
questions.sampleAnswer: Custom hooks extract reusable stateful logic. useDebounce delays a value update with useEffect and setTimeout cleanup. useTheme toggles light/dark and persists to localStorage. Components stay thin.

questions.question: Walk through how you would build today's machine coding app in 90 minutes.
questions.sampleAnswer: I scaffold Vite and router in 15 minutes. Login with controlled fields and fake submit next 25 minutes. Dashboard layout with sidebar, navbar, Outlet, two routes in 30 minutes. Wire navigation and fix build last 20 minutes. CSS stays minimal until core flows work.

questions.question: You finished machine coding but routing was shaky. How do you recover before Week 2?
questions.sampleAnswer: I spend one evening rebuilding only DashboardLayout + three routes from blank file without solutions. I read React Router nested routes tutorial and write route tree on paper. I drill Outlet until automatic, then continue Week 2 weather app where routing appears again.
